_note: PARTIAL, CROSS-SOURCE CORPUS — NOT the same integrity bar as clauses.json (manak). Values below describe ASHRAE TC9.9 'Thermal Guidelines for Data Processing Environments' (5th ed., 2021) Class A1/A2 envelopes. The primary ASHRAE book is commercially sold, not freely fetchable, and was NOT independently verified against a single primary document. These values are compiled from (a) one official ASHRAE-hosted free white paper covering the power-equipment section, cross-checked against (b) several independent secondary technical publications that converge on the same numbers. Every row carries source_type='cross_source_unverified' on its Citation so the UI/API can never present it as equivalent to a manak-verified clause. Scope: cooling/thermal envelope ONLY (temperature + a simplified relative-humidity band) — no electrical, fire, or DG-set commissioning coverage. See sitemind/docs/codes.txt for what's still needed to build the electrical/fire slice of this pillar.
known_simplification: ASHRAE's actual humidity criterion is a combined dew-point + RH% envelope (e.g. allowable A1/A2 minimum is 'the higher of -12C dew point or 8% RH', not a flat RH% floor). This corpus reduces that to a simplified RH%-only range because commissioning test logs typically report RH%, not dew point. A test record passing the simplified RH check is NOT a guarantee it would pass the true dew-point criterion — stated here, and repeated in the UI/quality-package banner.
sources_checked: https://www.ashrae.org/file%20library/technical%20resources/bookstore/ashrae_tc0909_power_white_paper_22_june_2016_revised.pdf (official ASHRAE, free, power-equipment section only — does not itself contain the full A1-A4 envelope table)

clauses.text: Recommended dry-bulb temperature range for IT equipment inlet air, all classes A1-A4: 18.0-27.0 degC (64.4-80.6 degF).
Allowable dry-bulb temperature range for Class A1 (enterprise servers): 15.0-32.0 degC (59-89.6 degF).
Allowable dry-bulb temperature range for Class A2 (volume servers): 10.0-35.0 degC (50-95 degF).

clauses.standard: ASHRAE TC9.9 (5th ed., 2021), cross-source compiled, SIMPLIFIED
clauses.text: Recommended humidity envelope (true criterion: dew point -9 to 15 degC DP, max 60% RH). SIMPLIFIED here to a flat relative-humidity range of 20-60% RH non-condensing for a test log that reports RH% only — the low end (20%) is this corpus's own practical floor, not an ASHRAE-stated number; the 60% max IS the ASHRAE-stated recommended ceiling.

clauses.standard: ASHRAE TC9.9 (5th ed., 2021), cross-source compiled, SIMPLIFIED
clauses.clause: Table 1 — Allowable envelope, Classes A1/A2, humidity (simplified)
clauses.text: Allowable humidity envelope for Classes A1/A2 (true criterion: min is the higher of -12 degC DP or 8% RH; max is 17 degC DP for A1 / 21 degC DP for A2, both capped at 80% RH). SIMPLIFIED here to a flat relative-humidity range of 8-80% RH non-condensing.
